title: MRL Increment 6: Filter by Requirement Type or Approval Status (Client-Side UI Only)
body:
MRL Increment 6: Filter by Requirement Type or Approval Status (Client-Side UI Only)
________________________________________
Background
With the Master Requirements List (MRL) now exposed via the api/mrl endpoint (Increment 4) and displayed in the UI (Increment 5), we are ready to enable simple, client-side filtering to improve usability.
This increment builds directly on the IMQ.Web changes introduced in Increment 5. It remains fully read-only and does not involve persistence, backend logic changes, or new schema elements.
________________________________________
Objective
Enable users to filter visible MRL entries in the UI by: - RequirementType (e.g., Education, Experience) - RequirementApprovalStatus (e.g., Approved, Draft)
________________________________________
Scope (In)
•	Add dropdown controls to the MRL view in IMQ.Web
•	Populate each dropdown with values from the corresponding enum
•	On selection change:
o	Call the appropriate existing API endpoint:
	/api/mrl/by-type/{requirementType}
	/api/mrl/by-status/{approvalStatus}
o	Update the visible table to reflect the filtered results
o	Selecting a new filter replaces the previous filter (no combinations)
o	Optionally include a “Show All” selection that reloads /api/mrl
•	Use the injected HttpClient in the Razor component (e.g., Http.GetFromJsonAsync<T>()).
o	Do not introduce JavaScript fetch() or browser-side filtering logic
________________________________________
Scope (Out)
•	No support for multi-filter combinations (e.g., type + status)
•	No client-side filtering or cache logic
•	No write operations (create/edit/delete)
•	No UI polish/styling beyond dropdown controls
•	No persistence or API changes
•	No auth or route protection logic
•	No DTO mapping — use domain models as returned by the API
________________________________________
Implementation Constraints
•	UI Project: Changes apply only to the IMQ.Web project
•	Routing: The MRL UI should be exposed via a dedicated route (e.g., /mrl) without modifying existing navigation unless explicitly required
•	Enum Binding:
o	Dropdown values should map directly to existing enum values (RequirementType, RequirementApprovalStatus)
o	Default enum binding behavior is sufficient — no custom parsing or formatting
•	HttpClient:
o	Use injected HttpClient in the Razor component
o	Do not hardcode base URLs or ports
o	API and UI run on separate ports in local dev
o	Base URL is environment-dependent and must match the running IMQ.Api instance (e.g., http://localhost:5091/api/mrl in local dev)
o	Prefer relative paths (e.g., /api/mrl, /api/mrl/by-type) assuming IMQ.Web proxies to IMQ.Api
________________________________________
Deliverables
•	Updated Razor component with two dropdown controls
o	Filter by RequirementType
o	Filter by RequirementApprovalStatus
•	Each dropdown triggers an API call on change
•	Filtered results replace existing result set (no append/merge)
•	Include a “Show All” option to reload /api/mrl
•	UI updates without full-page reload
________________________________________
Acceptance Criteria
•	Dropdowns correctly populate with enum values
•	API calls fire on dropdown change and update the list
•	Filtered list appears in the same table UI as Increment 5
•	Selecting “Show All” repopulates full MRL
•	No page reloads, no hardcoded ports, no JS fetch()
•	Selecting a new filter clears the previous one
•	The /mrl page remains read-only and non-editable
________________________________________
Tests
•	Manual validation only (no automated UI tests required for this increment)
•	Console should show correct HTTP calls and 200 OKs
•	Invalid enum values should return 400 (per backend default behavior)
________________________________________
Future Notes
•	Combined filter logic (type + status) may be added in a future increment
•	Enum-to-display-name mapping may be added via UI helpers later
•	A UI polish increment will follow this to improve visual structure and behavior
